title: Understanding Torque
text: To find the angular acceleration, we first need to calculate the torque exerted by the force. Torque (τ) is given by the formula: $$τ=F R$$, where F is the force applied and R is the radius of the grindstone.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Solid Disk
text: The moment of inertia (I) for a solid disk is calculated using the formula: $$I=\frac{1}{2} M R^2$$, where M is the mass and R is the radius of the disk.

title: Relating Torque and Angular Acceleration
text: The angular acceleration (\alpha) can be found using the relation: τ=I*\alpha, where τ is the torque and I is the moment of inertia. Rearrange this formula to solve for \alpha.

title: Calculate Torque
text: Using the formula $$τ=F R$$, calculate the torque exerted by the force of $$180$$ N on the grindstone with a radius of $$0.280$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Using the formula $$I=\frac{1}{2} M R^2$$, calculate the moment of inertia of the grindstone with a mass of $$75.0$$ kg and a radius of $$0.280$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Using the formula $$\alpha=\frac{\tau}{I}$$, calculate the angular acceleration of the grindstone.
